text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data that includes input examples and their correct outputs. For example, you might show it pictures of cats and dogs, each labeled correctly. Using this data, the system builds a model, which is like a set of rules it creates to recognize patterns. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. Finally, the accuracy of the model is checked by comparing its predictions to the correct answers. The higher the accuracy, the better the model has learned.
